name: Mall Food Court Altercation
scenarioDescription: You are a security guard at Southgate Centre mall. Two customers near the food court are shouting and shoving each other. The crowd is growing and bystanders are filming. You are the first guard on scene.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Mall Food Court Altercation.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Mall Food Court Altercation. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 70 reference.

parts.prompt: The crowd around the fight is growing fast. The manual identifies a specific first action when an incident involves a crowd. What is it?

manual_reference.excerpt: :  Obey all traffic laws when traveling to the location of the alarm; as a security professional you have no special authority to travel at a higher rate of speed or ignore traffic signal devices  Upon arrival at the scene check to see if doors and windows are secure; make note of any which appear to be open, unlocked, or otherwise unsecured  If you see evidence of a break in or other criminal activity, notify the police immediately; document your findings and take care no

choices.text: Announce loudly that police are on their way and that everyone must leave right away, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. A loud announcement can escalate crowd panic. The manual recommends de-escalation and calling backup, not triggering a stampede.

choices.text: Handle the crowd yourself first and call backup only if you cannot get the situation under control, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual says to call backup at the first sign of trouble — waiting until you are overwhelmed is too late and unsafe.

choices.text: Call for backup at the first sign of a crowd incident — do not wait for it to escalate before asking for help.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual states: 'Call for back-up at the first sign of an incident as it can turn from something minor into something quite large.' Backup must be called immediately, not after things worsen.

parts.prompt: The crowd is still growing. The manual describes a strategy for breaking down a crowd. What is the recommended approach?

manual_reference.excerpt: :  Obey all traffic laws when traveling to the location of the alarm; as a security professional you have no special authority to travel at a higher rate of speed or ignore traffic signal devices  Upon arrival at the scene check to see if doors and windows are secure; make note of any which appear to be open, unlocked, or otherwise unsecured  If you see evidence of a break in or other criminal activity, notify the police immediately; document your findings and take care no

choices.text: Try to remove the most vocal leaders of the crowd — de-escalating the leaders can calm the group as a whole.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual's strategy is: 'Remove the Leaders. One strategy you should keep in mind is to always try to de-escalate a crowd by removing its leaders.' Isolating the instigators drains the crowd's energy.

choices.text: Position yourself in the centre of the crowd to show authority and take control physically, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual warns that lone officers cannot contain an out-of-control crowd. Placing yourself in the middle is dangerous and ineffective.

choices.text: Tell bystanders they are legally required to leave and will be detained if they stay, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Bystanders have no legal obligation to leave a public area, and making false threats about detention can escalate the situation and expose you to liability.

name: Impaired Person Refusing to Leave a Venue
scenarioDescription: You are working security at a large concert venue. A man near the stage is acting erratically — swaying, slurring his words, bumping into guests, and shouting. Staff have asked him to leave twice and he refuses. Your supervisor directs you to deal with it.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Impaired Person Refusing to Leave a Venue.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Impaired Person Refusing to Leave a Venue. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 136 reference.

parts.prompt: Before approaching, you want to confirm the man is actually impaired and not experiencing a medical episode. Which combination of signs does the manual specifically list as indicators of substance impairment?

manual_reference.excerpt: responsibility to check all alarm indicators, but always start with the assumption the alarm is genuine  Contact your supervisor to advise you ar e leaving your post and to advise about the alarm  Determine the source of the alarm and the appropriate response o False alarm – follow the protocol provided in your post orders; report back to your supervisor o Emergency – follow the protocols provided in your post orders  Manage the scene and persons at the scene in a manner a

choices.text: Wearing sunglasses indoors and carrying an unusually large bag — signs the person is hiding something.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. These are loss-prevention indicators, not impairment signs. The manual's list of substance abuse indicators includes slurred speech, poor coordination, tremors, and bloodshot eyes.

choices.text: Refusing to answer questions and walking quickly away from security — signs of guilt and impairment, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. These behaviours may indicate awareness of security but are not on the manual's list of physical impairment signs. Behavioural signs include secretive behaviour, but the physical signs are different.

choices.text: Bloodshot eyes, slurred speech, poor coordination, and tremors — all listed as physical signs of impairment.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual lists bloodshot eyes, slurred speech, poor coordination, and tremors (shaking) as physical signs of substance abuse. Seeing these helps distinguish impairment from a medical condition like a stroke.

parts.prompt: You have confirmed he is impaired. He refuses to leave a third time and plants his feet. What is the appropriate next step according to the manual?

manual_reference.excerpt: responsibility to check all alarm indicators, but always start with the assumption the alarm is genuine  Contact your supervisor to advise you ar e leaving your post and to advise about the alarm  Determine the source of the alarm and the appropriate response o False alarm – follow the protocol provided in your post orders; report back to your supervisor o Emergency – follow the protocols provided in your post orders  Manage the scene and persons at the scene in a manner a

choices.text: Request police assistance and continue monitoring — do not escalate to physical force when verbal compliance has failed.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual says most situations can be handled through skilled communication, and escalating to force is risky. When verbal means are exhausted, police involvement is the correct next escalation.

choices.text: Use a restraint hold to guide him toward the exit since he has been given three chances to leave, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Section 26 of the Criminal Code makes every person criminally responsible for excessive force. A restraint on a non-violent impaired person who is only refusing to walk creates serious legal exposure.

choices.text: Leave him alone and return to your post since you cannot physically force him to move, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Abandoning an active situation without completing your duty is a conduct failure. Call police to assist — do not simply walk away.

name: Aggressive Visitor at a Hospital Entrance
scenarioDescription: You are posted at the main entrance of a regional hospital. A man arrives demanding to see a patient immediately but visiting hours ended 30 minutes ago. He raises his voice, slams his hand on the reception desk, and moves toward the restricted corridor.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Aggressive Visitor at a Hospital Entrance.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Aggressive Visitor at a Hospital Entrance. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 87 reference.

questions.title: Aggressive visitor: verbal control and access restriction

parts.prompt: The man is moving toward the restricted corridor. What should you do first?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 37 Conclusion The work of a security professional is diverse and challenging; no two shifts will be the same. Developing your observation and reporting skill sets will help you be effective in your duties. You will need to apply these skills to practical situations which occur in the course of your patrol, and to more complicated probl

choices.text: Step into his path, identify yourself, and calmly but firmly tell him he cannot proceed.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Verbal intervention and physical positioning are appropriate first steps before any force.

choices.text: right away tackle him to stop his movement, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Physical force is not justified unless there is an immediate threat of violence.

choices.text: Let him go through and monitor from a distance, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Allowing unauthorized access to a restricted hospital area is a safety and policy failure.

parts.prompt: He stops but begins yelling at you. What is the correct next action?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 37 Conclusion The work of a security professional is diverse and challenging; no two shifts will be the same. Developing your observation and reporting skill sets will help you be effective in your duties. You will need to apply these skills to practical situations which occur in the course of your patrol, and to more complicated probl

choices.text: Stay calm, lower your voice, acknowledge his concern, and explain how to arrange an emergency visit.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. De-escalation through empathy and clear information can resolve the situation without force.

choices.text: right away restrain him because he is yelling loudly, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Yelling alone does not justify physical restraint — verbal de-escalation should continue.

choices.text: Yell back at him to establish authority, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Matching aggression escalates the encounter and is unprofessional.

name: Suspicious Person at a Transit Hub
scenarioDescription: You are stationed at a light rail transit station during evening rush hour. A man is pacing erratically, talking loudly to himself, and has an oversized unattended bag. Several passengers have moved away from him and one person alerts you.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Suspicious Person at a Transit Hub.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Suspicious Person at a Transit Hub. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 51 reference.

questions.title: Suspicious subject: safety assessment and approach

parts.prompt: You see the man acting erratically. What should you do first?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 1 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures Now that you have been introduced to the security industry, and the various pieces of legislation which affect your work, it is time to look at how you will carry out your duties on a day-to-day basis. Learning Outcomes Upon completion of this module, you will be able to 1. Explain and demonstr

choices.text: Ask a nearby bystander to watch the bag while you investigate, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Bystanders must not be placed in potentially dangerous situations.

choices.text: Maintain a safe distance, observe the situation, radio your position to dispatch, and assess for hazards.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Safe positioning and dispatch communication come before direct engagement.

choices.text: Walk directly up to the man and demand he open his bag, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Rushing in without hazard assessment puts you and others at risk.

parts.prompt: You have assessed the scene. The man appears confused but not immediately violent. What now?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 1 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures Now that you have been introduced to the security industry, and the various pieces of legislation which affect your work, it is time to look at how you will carry out your duties on a day-to-day basis. Learning Outcomes Upon completion of this module, you will be able to 1. Explain and demonstr

choices.text: Physically restrain him until police arrive, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Restraint is not justified when there is no clear immediate threat.

choices.text: Approach calmly, introduce yourself, speak in a low tone, and ask if he is okay.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. A calm, low-key approach can reduce agitation and let you gather information safely.

choices.text: Evacuate the entire station and wait outside, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Proportionate response is required — a full evacuation overreacts to the situation.

name: Forced Entry at a Construction Site
scenarioDescription: You are on overnight patrol at a fenced construction site. Your motion sensor alert fires at 2:15 AM and you find the east gate padlock has been cut. You can see a light moving inside one of the unfinished structures.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Forced Entry at a Construction Site.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Forced Entry at a Construction Site. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 139 reference.

parts.prompt: You are at the forced gate and can see evidence of a break-in. What does the manual say you must do first upon finding a scene like this?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Six: Emergency Response Procedur es for Security Professionals, Page 10  Make note of occasions when you find st oves or hotplates left on when nobody is around; make note also if you see evidence of candles being used (many facilities do not permit burning of candles – check what the role is at your location). Include these occurrences in your shift report to your supervi

choices.text: Inspect every entrance to determine exactly how many people entered before calling anyone, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual says to notify police immediately and avoid disturbing the scene. A full solo perimeter check before calling in delays the response unnecessarily.

choices.text: Enter the site right away to locate and confront whoever is inside before they escape, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual requires you to notify police and secure your position first. Entering alone into an unknown situation without backup is unsafe and can destroy evidence.

choices.text: Stay outside, radio dispatch with your location, and notify police immediately — do not enter alone or disturb anything.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual states: if you see evidence of a break-in or criminal activity, notify the police immediately and take care not to displace or destroy evidence. Do not enter alone.

parts.prompt: Police and backup arrive. They find a teenager inside who got in on a dare and cooperates fully. What is the correct next step for you?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Six: Emergency Response Procedur es for Security Professionals, Page 10  Make note of occasions when you find st oves or hotplates left on when nobody is around; make note also if you see evidence of candles being used (many facilities do not permit burning of candles – check what the role is at your location). Include these occurrences in your shift report to your supervi

choices.text: Let them go without documenting since police are already on scene and no damage was done, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. You must document all security incidents, including trespass. Your report is your record regardless of what police do.

choices.text: Identify yourself, advise them they are trespassing, escort them out safely, and document the full incident in your notebook.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Lawful trespass notice, safe escort, and thorough documentation are all required steps — even for a cooperative minor.

choices.text: Physically detain the teenager until police decide what to do, regardless of their cooperation, to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Unnecessary physical detention of a cooperative person who is being escorted out can be unlawful and is disproportionate.

name: Aggressive Dog Loose on Patrol Route
scenarioDescription: You are on a foot patrol around a warehouse complex at night. As you round a corner, a large unleashed dog charges toward you from an open loading bay door. There is no handler visible and the dog is barking aggressively.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Aggressive Dog Loose on Patrol Route.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Aggressive Dog Loose on Patrol Route. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 122 reference.

parts.prompt: The dog is charging at you and there is no handler visible. What should you do first?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Five: Docum entation and Evidence, Page 16 Report #3 Incident Report Prairie Mall Date: Tuesday 5OCT2010 3000 River Avenue Time: 1116 approx Simpletown, AB Prepared by: Jane J. Officer I was on my patrol of the mall on Tuesday and was approached by Mrs. MEREDITH at approximately 1116. She had been entering the mall through the south doors at approximately 1105 and was appro

choices.text: Approach the dog to calm it with your hand, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Approaching an aggressive dog without a handler is dangerous.

choices.text: Back away slowly without running, avoid direct eye contact, and radio dispatch your exact location.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Slow withdrawal and communication protect your safety without provoking the animal.

choices.text: Run away as fast as possible, to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Running can trigger a chase response and increase the risk of attack.

parts.prompt: The dog retreats to the loading bay. What should you do next?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Five: Docum entation and Evidence, Page 16 Report #3 Incident Report Prairie Mall Date: Tuesday 5OCT2010 3000 River Avenue Time: 1116 approx Simpletown, AB Prepared by: Jane J. Officer I was on my patrol of the mall on Tuesday and was approached by Mrs. MEREDITH at approximately 1116. She had been entering the mall through the south doors at approximately 1105 and was appro

choices.text: Document the incident, notify dispatch of the open bay and the dog, and request animal control if needed.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Documentation and notification ensure the hazard is addressed and logged for follow-up.

choices.text: Continue your patrol through the same area right away, to keep the situation moving quickly.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The hazard has not been resolved — continue only once the area is confirmed safe.

choices.text: Enter the loading bay alone to close the door and secure the dog, to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Entering a confined space with an aggressive animal alone creates unnecessary risk.

name: Radio Blackout During Alarm Response
scenarioDescription: You are responding to a silent alarm at a downtown office tower at 11:50 PM. As you reach the stairwell on the fourth floor, your radio cuts out completely. You have your phone but cell signal shows one bar. You cannot confirm whether backup is coming.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Radio Blackout During Alarm Response.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Radio Blackout During Alarm Response. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 109 reference.

parts.prompt: Your radio is down and you cannot reach dispatch. What should you do?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Five: Docum entation and Evidence, Page 3 Notebooks As already stated in Module Three, you should have your notebook with you at all times when you are on duty. There are several reasons you should do so but some of the most important are that a notebook allows you to record events and related information at the time they occur and, your notes will be available in the futur

choices.text: Withdraw to a position with signal, attempt contact by phone, and wait for confirmation before proceeding alone.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Officer safety requires communication confirmation before solo entry into an unknown alarm situation.

choices.text: Reset the alarm panel yourself and leave the building, to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Resetting alarms without dispatch clearance can compromise the response and the investigation.

choices.text: Continue searching the building alone since you are already on-site, to keep the situation moving quickly.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Proceeding without communication in an unknown alarm situation is a serious safety risk.

parts.prompt: You reach a window with signal and contact dispatch. Backup is two minutes out. What is the best next action?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Five: Docum entation and Evidence, Page 3 Notebooks As already stated in Module Three, you should have your notebook with you at all times when you are on duty. There are several reasons you should do so but some of the most important are that a notebook allows you to record events and related information at the time they occur and, your notes will be available in the futur

choices.text: Post about the situation on the building staff group chat for speed, to keep the situation moving quickly.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Use only official communication channels for security incidents.

choices.text: Go back to your vehicle and wait there, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Abandoning your observed position loses important situational awareness.

choices.text: Hold your current position, maintain visual on the stairwell entry, and update dispatch every 60 seconds.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Holding position and maintaining communication keeps the response coordinated and safe.

name: Multi-Guard Coordination at a Parking Garage Fight
scenarioDescription: You are the lead guard on shift at a busy indoor parking garage. A fight is reported on Level 3 and two other guards are responding from different zones. You are at the command post monitoring CCTV and radio.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Multi-Guard Coordination at a Parking Garage Fight.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Multi-Guard Coordination at a Parking Garage Fight. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 66 reference.

parts.prompt: Both guards are heading to Level 3 from different directions. What should you do as the command post officer?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 16 Patrolling for Loss Prevention Loss prevention is the industry term for security professionals tasked with protecting the saleable goods in a retail setting. Stores will utilize different approaches to loss prevention based on their resources and needs; for example, large department stores usually have more revenue to allocate to se

choices.text: Stay silent and let the responding guards figure it out themselves, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The command post role requires active coordination, not passive observation.

choices.text: Direct each guard to approach from opposite ends, share live camera observations, and update them continuously.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Coordinated approach and live CCTV updates improve safety and ensure full coverage.

choices.text: Leave the command post and join the response personally, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Abandoning the command post removes dispatch oversight and scene coordination.

parts.prompt: The situation is resolved. One guard detained a person and the other assisted an injured victim. What is your next step?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 16 Patrolling for Loss Prevention Loss prevention is the industry term for security professionals tasked with protecting the saleable goods in a retail setting. Stores will utilize different approaches to loss prevention based on their resources and needs; for example, large department stores usually have more revenue to allocate to se

choices.text: Tell each guard to write their own separate report with no shared information, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Coordinated documentation ensures consistency and an accurate incident record.

choices.text: Document the full timeline, camera footage references, all communications, and each guard's actions.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Complete incident documentation from all roles is required for accountability and legal review.

choices.text: Delete the CCTV footage now that the situation is resolved, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. CCTV footage is critical evidence and must be preserved, not deleted.

name: Unclear Dispatch Instructions During Fire Alarm
scenarioDescription: A fire alarm activates at a large office complex where you are working. Dispatch calls you on the radio but the message cuts out mid-sentence. You hear 'evacuate' and 'east wing' but nothing else. Staff are beginning to panic in the corridor.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Unclear Dispatch Instructions During Fire Alarm.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Unclear Dispatch Instructions During Fire Alarm. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 6 reference.

parts.prompt: You only caught part of the dispatch message. Staff are looking at you for direction. What should you do first?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Introduction, Page 2 4. Apply a professional and effective communication style for managing and controlling incidents 5. Demonstrate proficiency and accuracy in note-taking and report writing 6. Explain how to manage an emergency situation 7. Maintain personal safety and wellness while working as a security professional This course is delivered in seven modules; each module deals

choices.text: Wait in place until you receive a complete message before doing anything, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Delaying evacuation during a fire alarm to get a perfect message puts people at risk.

choices.text: Tell staff to go back to their desks since the message was unclear, to keep the situation moving quickly.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. When in doubt during a fire alarm, treat it as real and begin evacuation.

choices.text: Begin guiding staff toward the nearest exit while trying to re-establish radio contact to confirm the full message.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Acting on the confirmed information you have — evacuation — while seeking clarification is the right balance.

parts.prompt: You have guided the east wing to the exit. You re-establish radio contact. What should you report to dispatch?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Introduction, Page 2 4. Apply a professional and effective communication style for managing and controlling incidents 5. Demonstrate proficiency and accuracy in note-taking and report writing 6. Explain how to manage an emergency situation 7. Maintain personal safety and wellness while working as a security professional This course is delivered in seven modules; each module deals

choices.text: Your name, location, the number of people evacuated, and request confirmation of the full situation.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Clear status reports with location and headcount help dispatch manage the full response.

choices.text: Tell dispatch everything is fine and you handled it without further detail, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Dispatch needs detailed updates to coordinate the broader response.

choices.text: Wait for dispatch to call you rather than initiating contact, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Proactive communication during an active emergency is part of your role.

name: Shoplifting Stop at a Retail Store
scenarioDescription: You are a loss prevention officer at a large clothing retailer. You have been watching a man on CCTV who appears furtive — glancing at cameras, wearing an unusually bulky coat on a warm day, and carrying a large backpack. He conceals two items inside the backpack and is now walking toward the exit.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Shoplifting Stop at a Retail Store.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Shoplifting Stop at a Retail Store. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 138 reference.

questions.title: Loss prevention: detention timing and documentation

parts.prompt: The man has concealed the items but is still inside the store near the last cashier. Should you stop him now?

manual_reference.excerpt: cape routes for all areas.  Ask if there are individuals within the building who may need assistance during an emergency situation; this may include persons in wheelchairs, individuals with health problems, and the visually or hearing impaired.  Report hazards when you find them; overloaded plug sockets, uncleared piles of garbage, space heaters placed close to furniture, window coverings, or other items are unsightly (they take away from the professional appearance of the

choices.text: Yes — you already have video evidence so it doesn't matter where you stop him, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Where you stop him affects whether the theft is legally complete. The manual is clear: wait until he is past all cashiers and outside.

choices.text: Yes — confront him now before he has a chance to run, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual says the offence of theft should not be considered complete until the subject passes all cashiers and has left the store. Detaining him inside gives him a legal defence.

choices.text: No — wait until he has passed all cashiers and left the store before approaching him.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The Alberta manual states theft is not considered complete until the subject has passed all checkout areas and left the store. Stopping earlier lets him claim he intended to pay.

parts.prompt: He has exited. You identify yourself and he cooperates. Your supervisor is on the way. What should your notebook notes include?

manual_reference.excerpt: cape routes for all areas.  Ask if there are individuals within the building who may need assistance during an emergency situation; this may include persons in wheelchairs, individuals with health problems, and the visually or hearing impaired.  Report hazards when you find them; overloaded plug sockets, uncleared piles of garbage, space heaters placed close to furniture, window coverings, or other items are unsightly (they take away from the professional appearance of the

choices.text: The time, location, a factual description of what you observed on camera, his behaviour, and your actions — no opinions.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual states notes must record facts, not opinions or conclusions. Write what you observed, not what you think about the person.

choices.text: Your opinion that he is a repeat offender and seemed nervous, plus the camera reference, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual says do not write opinions or conclusions that cannot be proven. Stick to factual observations only.

choices.text: A brief summary now and a full detailed account later from memory once you are less stressed, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual says notes must be made at the time events occur — not reconstructed later from memory, which is less accurate.

name: Vandalism Discovered During After-Hours Patrol
scenarioDescription: You are doing an overnight patrol at a school. At 1:30 AM you discover extensive graffiti and two broken windows in the east corridor. The damage appears recent and there are spray paint cans sitting on the floor.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Vandalism Discovered During After-Hours Patrol.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Vandalism Discovered During After-Hours Patrol. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 137 reference.

questions.title: Scene preservation: evidence rules on criminal activity

parts.prompt: The manual has a specific rule about what to do when you find evidence of criminal activity during a patrol. What does it say?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Jan-14 Docu Em When secur action mean and c perso peop emer Upo wind to be a Solicitor Genera ument events ergency n an emerge rity of the sc ns. Regardle ns you must continually o ons and prop le to arrive o rgency. Pers n arrival at t dows are sec e open, unlo If you see e activity, no findings an evidence. Be co premi I ( p s al and Ministry of s in your not y Scene ency situatio cene. Emerg ess of the ro be calm, uti observant. D perty you are on scene if, sons on

choices.text: Notify the police immediately and take care not to displace or destroy any evidence.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual states exactly: 'If you see evidence of a break-in or other criminal activity, notify the police immediately; document your findings and take care not to displace or destroy evidence.'

choices.text: Pick up identifiable items like the spray cans as evidence to hand to police when they arrive.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Touching and moving items is displacing evidence, which the manual explicitly warns against. Leave everything in place and let police process the scene.

choices.text: Document everything and clean up the scene so it is safe before police arrive, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Cleaning up is exactly what the manual prohibits — you must not displace or destroy evidence. Preserve the scene as found.

parts.prompt: Police have been called and are 10 minutes away. What should you do while you wait at the scene?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Jan-14 Docu Em When secur action mean and c perso peop emer Upo wind to be a Solicitor Genera ument events ergency n an emerge rity of the sc ns. Regardle ns you must continually o ons and prop le to arrive o rgency. Pers n arrival at t dows are sec e open, unlo If you see e activity, no findings an evidence. Be co premi I ( p s al and Ministry of s in your not y Scene ency situatio cene. Emerg ess of the ro be calm, uti observant. D perty you are on scene if, sons on

choices.text: Secure the perimeter to stop anyone else from entering, and write a detailed factual account of exactly what you found and when.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Scene security and contemporaneous notes are both required. Your written record of what you found — before police arrive — is valuable evidence.

choices.text: Start photographing and then post the images online so administration can see what happened overnight, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual says photos or video obtained during an investigation must not be released to media or posted on social networking sites.

choices.text: Wait until after you speak with police to write your notes, so you can include what they tell you, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Notes must record what you personally observed at the time. Write them immediately — before your memory fades and before talking to others.

name: Vehicle Break-In Found in a Parking Lot
scenarioDescription: You are patrolling a commercial parking lot and find a vehicle with its window smashed. Glass is on the ground and a bag appears to be missing from the back seat. A woman nearby says she saw a man running toward the east exit 5 minutes ago.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Vehicle Break-In Found in a Parking Lot.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Vehicle Break-In Found in a Parking Lot. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 69 reference.

questions.title: Evidence: vehicle break-in witness and scene

parts.prompt: You find the broken vehicle. What should you do first?

manual_reference.excerpt: ividual’s consent should be used only for the purposes of the investigation. Under no circumstances should you release photos or video footage to the media, or use such material in any other manner (e.g., social networking sites, the Internet). Alarms Another form of surveillance is the monitoring of alarms. Most premises have fire alarms on site, and an increasing number of locations have “burglar” or security alarm systems in place. When assuming your post, take time to fin

choices.text: Do not move the vehicle or glass. Note the time, location, plate number, and call dispatch.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Preserving the scene and notifying dispatch are the immediate priorities.

choices.text: Move the vehicle to a safer spot to avoid further incidents, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Moving the vehicle disturbs the evidence scene.

choices.text: Chase the suspect toward the east exit right away, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Leaving an unsecured scene and pursuing a suspect alone creates safety and evidence risks.

parts.prompt: The vehicle owner arrives and is upset. She wants to know everything right away. What should you do?

manual_reference.excerpt: ividual’s consent should be used only for the purposes of the investigation. Under no circumstances should you release photos or video footage to the media, or use such material in any other manner (e.g., social networking sites, the Internet). Alarms Another form of surveillance is the monitoring of alarms. Most premises have fire alarms on site, and an increasing number of locations have “burglar” or security alarm systems in place. When assuming your post, take time to fin

choices.text: Stay calm, explain that police have been called, take her statement, and add it to your incident notes.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Gathering the witness statement promptly and keeping her informed is good practice.

choices.text: Tell her to wait until police arrive and refuse to speak with her, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. You should acknowledge her, take her statement, and keep her informed within appropriate limits.

choices.text: Share all investigation details with her right away to help her feel better, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Details of an active investigation should be shared only through proper channels.

name: Trespasser Refusing to Leave Private Property
scenarioDescription: You are working the gate at a private industrial park. A man on foot has entered through a gap in the perimeter fence and is in a restricted zone. When you approach, he claims he has a right to be there and refuses to identify himself or leave.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Trespasser Refusing to Leave Private Property.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Trespasser Refusing to Leave Private Property. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 35 reference.

questions.title: Trespass: Petty Trespass Act and escalation steps

parts.prompt: Under the Alberta Petty Trespass Act, what is a person required to do when directed to leave by a security officer authorized by the owner?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Oct-14 Module Two: The Canadian Legal System and Security Professionals, Page 10 Individuals may grant permission for the search, and then change their mind. At such point as they advise you they would like you to stop searching, you must stop immediately. If you have arrested an individual in accordance with section 494, you may search the individual if you have reason to believe they a

choices.text: Leave immediately after being directed — there is no right to stay and argue once told to leave.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The Petty Trespass Act states a person 'does not leave land immediately after he or she is directed to do so by the owner or occupier or a person authorized by them' is guilty of an offence. Immediate compliance is required.

choices.text: They have the right to ask for a written notice before they are required to leave, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The Petty Trespass Act requires the person to leave immediately after a verbal direction from an authorized person. No written notice is required for this obligation to apply.

choices.text: They may stay if they can provide a reasonable explanation for their presence, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Once directed to leave by an authorized person, the person must leave immediately under the Petty Trespass Act. Their reason for being there is not a defence.

parts.prompt: He still refuses to leave after two verbal directions. The manual describes an escalation process for persistent non-compliance. What is the correct next step?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Oct-14 Module Two: The Canadian Legal System and Security Professionals, Page 10 Individuals may grant permission for the search, and then change their mind. At such point as they advise you they would like you to stop searching, you must stop immediately. If you have arrested an individual in accordance with section 494, you may search the individual if you have reason to believe they a

choices.text: Call your supervisor or designated contact for direction, document the encounter, and if he continues, advise him you will treat this as a trespass requiring police.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual says if an individual is persistent and keeps you from your duties, call your supervisor. If they still will not leave, advise them you will treat the incident as trespassing and involve police.

choices.text: Do nothing further since you have already given two warnings and your authority is exhausted, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual is clear — if a person is persistent, you escalate to your supervisor and then to police. Walking away abandons your post and duty.

choices.text: Physically force him off the property yourself since the Trespass Act gives you full authority to remove him, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The Petty Trespass Act does not give security guards the right to physically drag a person off property. Police involvement is the required escalation.

name: Media Crew Arriving at an Active Incident Scene
scenarioDescription: You are guarding a perimeter around a fire that has broken out in a loading dock at a commercial property. Two reporters and a camera crew approach and demand access to film the scene. Police have not yet arrived on site.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Media Crew Arriving at an Active Incident Scene.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Media Crew Arriving at an Active Incident Scene. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 14 reference.

questions.title: Media access: scene control and legal limits

parts.prompt: A reporter demands entry to the restricted scene. What should you do?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module One: Introduction to the Security Industry, Page 6 To support the requirement for licensing, Alberta has implemented application procedures for both individuals and businesses. Any individual wishing to apply for a security professional licence in Alberta must meet the following requirements:  18 years of age at the time of application  Canadian citizen or legally entitle

choices.text: Let them in since it is a public interest story, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. A restricted scene on private property can be maintained regardless of public interest claims.

choices.text: Give a brief statement on camera to appear cooperative, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Unauthorized statements to media can compromise an active incident response.

choices.text: Politely but firmly deny entry, explain the scene is restricted for safety, and direct them to the property's communications contact.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Denying unauthorized access and directing media through proper channels protects the scene.

parts.prompt: The reporter says they have a legal right to film from the public sidewalk nearby. What should you do?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module One: Introduction to the Security Industry, Page 6 To support the requirement for licensing, Alberta has implemented application procedures for both individuals and businesses. Any individual wishing to apply for a security professional licence in Alberta must meet the following requirements:  18 years of age at the time of application  Canadian citizen or legally entitle

choices.text: Call police to have them arrested for filming near the scene, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Filming from a public sidewalk is generally lawful — this request would not be appropriate.

choices.text: Block their camera with your body or hand, to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Interfering with lawful media activity on public property can create legal issues for you.

choices.text: Acknowledge their right to film from public areas, and firmly maintain your restricted perimeter.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. You cannot prevent filming from public property, but you control your restricted zone.

name: Person Demanding to Know Why They Were Searched
scenarioDescription: At a bar entrance, you conduct a routine bag check and find nothing prohibited. The guest becomes angry and demands to know your legal basis for searching them. They say they will sue and begin recording on their phone.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Person Demanding to Know Why They Were Searched.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Person Demanding to Know Why They Were Searched. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 85 reference.

questions.title: Authority: lawful search and rights explanation

parts.prompt: The guest is angry and recording you. What should you do first?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 35 Excited Delirium There is a rare medical condition which causes individuals to behave in a manner that is very disorderly and violent. The condition is known as excited delirium and is known to occur as a result of the affected individual being restrained, as happens when a person is arrested or detained. While it is likely you will

choices.text: Demand they stop recording before you explain anything, to keep the situation moving quickly.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Making demands before explaining yourself escalates the situation unnecessarily.

choices.text: Stay calm, explain that the search is a condition of entry on private property, and that they may decline and leave.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Clear explanation of the legal basis for a consent-based search at entry is the right response.

choices.text: Grab their phone to stop them from recording, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Interfering with someone recording in a public or semi-public space is not within your authority.

parts.prompt: They continue to argue and say you violated their rights. What is the best next step?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 35 Excited Delirium There is a rare medical condition which causes individuals to behave in a manner that is very disorderly and violent. The condition is known as excited delirium and is known to occur as a result of the affected individual being restrained, as happens when a person is arrested or detained. While it is likely you will

choices.text: Apologize and tell them the search was not required after all, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Inconsistent explanations undermine your credibility and the property's policy.

choices.text: Tell them they are under arrest for obstruction, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. A verbal disagreement does not meet the grounds for arrest.

choices.text: Acknowledge their concern, reiterate the property's search policy, and offer to have a supervisor speak with them.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Escalating to a supervisor for a dispute about policy is the appropriate, professional step.

name: Bomb Threat Call at a Shopping Centre
scenarioDescription: You are working the main security desk at a large shopping centre. At 2:45 PM you receive a phone call from an unknown person who says there is a bomb in the building and gives a 20-minute countdown before hanging up.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Bomb Threat Call at a Shopping Centre.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Bomb Threat Call at a Shopping Centre. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 80 reference.

parts.prompt: The caller just hung up. What should you do immediately?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 30 The post orders you learned about above provide a way to transfer information amongst personnel in a consistent and organized manner; start by reviewing the orders with the individual whom you are relieving. Ask if there were any incidents in the previous shift or whether or not there is anything specific to be watching for (e.g., i

choices.text: Dismiss it as a prank and wait to see if anything happens, to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Every bomb threat must be treated as real until authorities determine otherwise.

choices.text: Note the exact time, words used, voice description, and immediately alert your supervisor and call 911.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Preserving call details and immediately alerting supervisors and emergency services is the correct first action.

choices.text: Announce over the PA system that there is a bomb threat to warn shoppers, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Public announcements should only be made under direction from emergency services or management to avoid panic.

parts.prompt: Emergency services are responding and your supervisor asks you to support the evacuation. What is most important?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 30 The post orders you learned about above provide a way to transfer information amongst personnel in a consistent and organized manner; start by reviewing the orders with the individual whom you are relieving. Ask if there were any incidents in the previous shift or whether or not there is anything specific to be watching for (e.g., i

choices.text: Follow the site emergency plan, guide people to exits calmly, and avoid using elevators.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Following the emergency plan and guiding calm evacuation is your role in this situation.

choices.text: Search the building yourself to find the device before police arrive, to keep the situation moving quickly.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Bomb searches must only be performed by trained emergency personnel — never security guards.

choices.text: Lock the building entrances to contain the threat, to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Locking people inside during a bomb threat creates serious danger.

name: Witnessing Coworker Accept a Bribe at the Loading Dock
scenarioDescription: During your break, you observe a fellow security guard accept cash from a delivery driver at the loading dock and wave him through without completing the required vehicle log. The guard notices you watching and says to forget about it.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Witnessing Coworker Accept a Bribe at the Loading Dock.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Witnessing Coworker Accept a Bribe at the Loading Dock. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 79 reference.

parts.prompt: Your coworker tells you to stay quiet about what you saw. What should you do?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 29 Post Orders A post order is a document which outlines your duties and responsibilities during your shift. Your employer will provide you with the post order information at the outset of your shift; read them through each time, as there may be changes or updated information which you will need to know in order to carry out your dutie

choices.text: Confront the coworker aggressively about what you saw, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Direct confrontation can create conflict and evidence problems. Use official reporting channels instead.

choices.text: Document what you witnessed with times and details, and report it to your supervisor through the official process.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Security personnel have a professional duty to report misconduct through proper channels.

choices.text: Agree to stay quiet since you do not want conflict with your coworker, to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Ignoring misconduct can make you complicit and violates professional standards.

parts.prompt: You have made your report. Your supervisor thanks you and asks you not to discuss it with others. What is the correct next step?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 29 Post Orders A post order is a document which outlines your duties and responsibilities during your shift. Your employer will provide you with the post order information at the outset of your shift; read them through each time, as there may be changes or updated information which you will need to know in order to carry out your dutie

choices.text: Keep the matter confidential, allow the investigation to proceed, and maintain your professionalism at work.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Confidentiality and professionalism protect the investigation and your position.

choices.text: Tell other coworkers what you reported to warn them, to keep the situation moving quickly.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Discussing an active investigation can compromise it and create a hostile work environment.

choices.text: Withdraw your report to avoid making things awkward, to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Reports of misconduct should not be withdrawn under social pressure.

name: Sleepy Guard Abandons Post Mid-Shift
scenarioDescription: You arrive for your shift and discover the guard you are relieving has left the post 30 minutes early without signing off in the log. The entrance has been unmonitored and there is no note explaining where they went.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Sleepy Guard Abandons Post Mid-Shift.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Sleepy Guard Abandons Post Mid-Shift. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 76 reference.

questions.title: Conduct: abandoned post and handoff failure

parts.prompt: The post has been unmonitored for 30 minutes. What should you do first?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 26 Interacting with the Media As you are reminded several times throughout this course, your primary duty is to safeguard the persons or property as directed by your employer. On occasion, incidents which attract media attention may occur at your premises. For example, an executive working in an office which is housed in the office bui

choices.text: Wait for the other guard to return before assuming control, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The post needs immediate coverage — waiting leaves it unmonitored further.

choices.text: Immediately take control of the post, check for any unreported incidents, and notify your supervisor of the gap.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Securing the post and reporting the gap are the immediate priorities.

choices.text: Leave the post yourself since the other guard is not following policy anyway, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Two wrongs do not make a right — you must fulfill your duty.

parts.prompt: Your supervisor asks you to document what happened. The manual has specific rules about what belongs in a security notebook. Which entry follows those rules correctly?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 26 Interacting with the Media As you are reminded several times throughout this course, your primary duty is to safeguard the persons or property as directed by your employer. On occasion, incidents which attract media attention may occur at your premises. For example, an executive working in an office which is housed in the office bui

choices.text: 'Guard Smith clearly abandoned the post and is not suited for this job. I arrived at 1900 and had to clean up his mess again.', to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual explicitly says do not write opinions or conclusions that cannot be proven. Writing that someone 'is not suited for the job' is an opinion that does not belong in a security notebook.

choices.text: 'Arrived 1900. Post unoccupied. Log shows last entry 1826 by Guard Smith. No note left. Notified Supervisor Davis at 1902. Assumed post 1902.' — factual, time-stamped, no opinion.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual says notebook entries must record facts — who, what, where, when — with no opinions or conclusions. This entry does exactly that. The manual also says do not write anything you would not want your employer, the police, or the court to read.

choices.text: 'Post was empty when I arrived. I took over and everything is fine now.' — brief but covers the basics, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. This entry omits key details the manual requires: exact times, log entry references, and supervisory notification. Brief notes that leave out facts are not sufficient for accountability or legal purposes.

name: Mall Food Court Altercation
scenarioDescription: You are a security guard at Southgate Centre mall. Two customers near the food court are shouting and shoving each other. The crowd is growing and bystanders are filming. You are the first guard on scene.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Mall Food Court Altercation.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Mall Food Court Altercation. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 75 reference.

parts.prompt: The crowd around the fight is growing fast. The manual identifies a specific first action when an incident involves a crowd. What is it?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 25 Physical Behavioural Psychological  Blood shot eyes  Pupils larger or smaller than usual  Changes in appetite or sleep patterns; sudden weight loss or gain  Deteriorated physical appearance, poor grooming  Unusual smells on breath, body, or clothing  Tremors (shaking), slurred speech, poor coordination  Poor attendance at wor

choices.text: Handle the crowd yourself first and call backup only if you cannot get the situation under control, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual says to call backup at the first sign of trouble — waiting until you are overwhelmed is too late and unsafe.

choices.text: Announce loudly that police are on their way and that everyone must leave right away, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. A loud announcement can escalate crowd panic. The manual recommends de-escalation and calling backup, not triggering a stampede.

choices.text: Call for backup at the first sign of a crowd incident — do not wait for it to escalate before asking for help.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual states: 'Call for back-up at the first sign of an incident as it can turn from something minor into something quite large.' Backup must be called immediately, not after things worsen.

parts.prompt: The crowd is still growing. The manual describes a strategy for breaking down a crowd. What is the recommended approach?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 25 Physical Behavioural Psychological  Blood shot eyes  Pupils larger or smaller than usual  Changes in appetite or sleep patterns; sudden weight loss or gain  Deteriorated physical appearance, poor grooming  Unusual smells on breath, body, or clothing  Tremors (shaking), slurred speech, poor coordination  Poor attendance at wor

choices.text: Position yourself in the centre of the crowd to show authority and take control physically, to show authority in front of bystanders.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual warns that lone officers cannot contain an out-of-control crowd. Placing yourself in the middle is dangerous and ineffective.

choices.text: Tell bystanders they are legally required to leave and will be detained if they stay, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Bystanders have no legal obligation to leave a public area, and making false threats about detention can escalate the situation and expose you to liability.

choices.text: Try to remove the most vocal leaders of the crowd — de-escalating the leaders can calm the group as a whole.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual's strategy is: 'Remove the Leaders. One strategy you should keep in mind is to always try to de-escalate a crowd by removing its leaders.' Isolating the instigators drains the crowd's energy.

name: Impaired Person Refusing to Leave a Venue
scenarioDescription: You are working security at a large concert venue. A man near the stage is acting erratically — swaying, slurring his words, bumping into guests, and shouting. Staff have asked him to leave twice and he refuses. Your supervisor directs you to deal with it.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Impaired Person Refusing to Leave a Venue.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Impaired Person Refusing to Leave a Venue. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 65 reference.

parts.prompt: Before approaching, you want to confirm the man is actually impaired and not experiencing a medical episode. Which combination of signs does the manual specifically list as indicators of substance impairment?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 15 Follow a consistent route through or around the premises; you should make a routine to ensure you have covered all areas you are responsible for. Be careful, however, not to become so familiar with your route that you stop looking closely enough to notice when things are amiss. Check doors and windows as you walk by; if they should

choices.text: Bloodshot eyes, slurred speech, poor coordination, and tremors — all listed as physical signs of impairment.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual lists bloodshot eyes, slurred speech, poor coordination, and tremors (shaking) as physical signs of substance abuse. Seeing these helps distinguish impairment from a medical condition like a stroke.

choices.text: Refusing to answer questions and walking quickly away from security — signs of guilt and impairment, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. These behaviours may indicate awareness of security but are not on the manual's list of physical impairment signs. Behavioural signs include secretive behaviour, but the physical signs are different.

choices.text: Wearing sunglasses indoors and carrying an unusually large bag — signs the person is hiding something.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. These are loss-prevention indicators, not impairment signs. The manual's list of substance abuse indicators includes slurred speech, poor coordination, tremors, and bloodshot eyes.

parts.prompt: You have confirmed he is impaired. He refuses to leave a third time and plants his feet. What is the appropriate next step according to the manual?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 15 Follow a consistent route through or around the premises; you should make a routine to ensure you have covered all areas you are responsible for. Be careful, however, not to become so familiar with your route that you stop looking closely enough to notice when things are amiss. Check doors and windows as you walk by; if they should

choices.text: Request police assistance and continue monitoring — do not escalate to physical force when verbal compliance has failed.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual says most situations can be handled through skilled communication, and escalating to force is risky. When verbal means are exhausted, police involvement is the correct next escalation.

choices.text: Leave him alone and return to your post since you cannot physically force him to move, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Abandoning an active situation without completing your duty is a conduct failure. Call police to assist — do not simply walk away.

choices.text: Use a restraint hold to guide him toward the exit since he has been given three chances to leave, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Section 26 of the Criminal Code makes every person criminally responsible for excessive force. A restraint on a non-violent impaired person who is only refusing to walk creates serious legal exposure.

name: Aggressive Visitor at a Hospital Entrance
scenarioDescription: You are posted at the main entrance of a regional hospital. A man arrives demanding to see a patient immediately but visiting hours ended 30 minutes ago. He raises his voice, slams his hand on the reception desk, and moves toward the restricted corridor.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Aggressive Visitor at a Hospital Entrance.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Aggressive Visitor at a Hospital Entrance. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 62 reference.

questions.title: Aggressive visitor: verbal control and access restriction

parts.prompt: The man is moving toward the restricted corridor. What should you do first?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 12 Physical preparedness Take stock of the following prior to starting your shift:  Are you feeling well? o Take care of your health by eating right and exercising regularly o Make sure you get enough sleep o Going to work when you are ill is a hazard to yourself and to those you work with; if you are sick, call your supervisor  Unif

choices.text: Step into his path, identify yourself, and calmly but firmly tell him he cannot proceed.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Verbal intervention and physical positioning are appropriate first steps before any force.

choices.text: right away tackle him to stop his movement, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Physical force is not justified unless there is an immediate threat of violence.

choices.text: Let him go through and monitor from a distance, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Allowing unauthorized access to a restricted hospital area is a safety and policy failure.

parts.prompt: He stops but begins yelling at you. What is the correct next action?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 12 Physical preparedness Take stock of the following prior to starting your shift:  Are you feeling well? o Take care of your health by eating right and exercising regularly o Make sure you get enough sleep o Going to work when you are ill is a hazard to yourself and to those you work with; if you are sick, call your supervisor  Unif

choices.text: Stay calm, lower your voice, acknowledge his concern, and explain how to arrange an emergency visit.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. De-escalation through empathy and clear information can resolve the situation without force.

choices.text: Yell back at him to establish authority, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Matching aggression escalates the encounter and is unprofessional.

choices.text: right away restrain him because he is yelling loudly, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Yelling alone does not justify physical restraint — verbal de-escalation should continue.

name: Suspicious Person at a Transit Hub
scenarioDescription: You are stationed at a light rail transit station during evening rush hour. A man is pacing erratically, talking loudly to himself, and has an oversized unattended bag. Several passengers have moved away from him and one person alerts you.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Suspicious Person at a Transit Hub.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Suspicious Person at a Transit Hub. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 61 reference.

questions.title: Suspicious subject: safety assessment and approach

parts.prompt: You see the man acting erratically. What should you do first?

choices.text: Ask a nearby bystander to watch the bag while you investigate, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Bystanders must not be placed in potentially dangerous situations.

choices.text: Walk directly up to the man and demand he open his bag, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Rushing in without hazard assessment puts you and others at risk.

choices.text: Maintain a safe distance, observe the situation, radio your position to dispatch, and assess for hazards.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Safe positioning and dispatch communication come before direct engagement.

parts.prompt: You have assessed the scene. The man appears confused but not immediately violent. What now?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Jan-14 Conc exam chang moto Ass o For e that t work same Repe beco each of ea able t What Witho buildi finish Pat Patro prote sight How e involv prope the p Befo r out yo seem prope of be duties a Solicitor Genera centrate on mple, if you h ges in the vo r stops. ociating info example, if a to someone at Mega Ma e place as m eat the inform me to do.
