choices.text: Approach calmly, introduce yourself, speak in a low tone, and ask if he is okay.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. A calm, low-key approach can reduce agitation and let you gather information safely.

choices.text: Physically restrain him until police arrive, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Restraint is not justified when there is no clear immediate threat.

choices.text: Evacuate the entire station and wait outside, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Proportionate response is required — a full evacuation overreacts to the situation.

name: Forced Entry at a Construction Site
scenarioDescription: You are on overnight patrol at a fenced construction site. Your motion sensor alert fires at 2:15 AM and you find the east gate padlock has been cut. You can see a light moving inside one of the unfinished structures.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Forced Entry at a Construction Site.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Forced Entry at a Construction Site. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 60 reference.

parts.prompt: You are at the forced gate and can see evidence of a break-in. What does the manual say you must do first upon finding a scene like this?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 10 There are other instances when your ability to accurately interpret information will be distorted by other factors. For example, individuals who have had strokes or other types of brain injury may have slurred speech and uncoordinated movement. They may appear intoxicated, but the reality is their physical impairment causes them to

choices.text: Inspect every entrance to determine exactly how many people entered before calling anyone, so the scene does not get more chaotic.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual says to notify police immediately and avoid disturbing the scene. A full solo perimeter check before calling in delays the response unnecessarily.

choices.text: Enter the site right away to locate and confront whoever is inside before they escape, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The manual requires you to notify police and secure your position first. Entering alone into an unknown situation without backup is unsafe and can destroy evidence.

choices.text: Stay outside, radio dispatch with your location, and notify police immediately — do not enter alone or disturb anything.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. The manual states: if you see evidence of a break-in or criminal activity, notify the police immediately and take care not to displace or destroy evidence. Do not enter alone.

parts.prompt: Police and backup arrive. They find a teenager inside who got in on a dare and cooperates fully. What is the correct next step for you?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Module Three: Basic Security Procedures, Page 10 There are other instances when your ability to accurately interpret information will be distorted by other factors. For example, individuals who have had strokes or other types of brain injury may have slurred speech and uncoordinated movement. They may appear intoxicated, but the reality is their physical impairment causes them to

choices.text: Physically detain the teenager until police decide what to do, regardless of their cooperation, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Unnecessary physical detention of a cooperative person who is being escorted out can be unlawful and is disproportionate.

choices.text: Identify yourself, advise them they are trespassing, escort them out safely, and document the full incident in your notebook.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Lawful trespass notice, safe escort, and thorough documentation are all required steps — even for a cooperative minor.

choices.text: Let them go without documenting since police are already on scene and no damage was done, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. You must document all security incidents, including trespass. Your report is your record regardless of what police do.

name: Aggressive Dog Loose on Patrol Route
scenarioDescription: You are on a foot patrol around a warehouse complex at night. As you round a corner, a large unleashed dog charges toward you from an open loading bay door. There is no handler visible and the dog is barking aggressively.
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Scenario — Aggressive Dog Loose on Patrol Route.
AI Coach: Think about lawful authority, de-escalation, and documentation.
AI Coach: Apply what you know from the manual — safety first, then procedure.
imagePrompt: Realistic Alberta security training scene: Aggressive Dog Loose on Patrol Route. Security guard in uniform, professional setting. Manual page 52 reference.

parts.prompt: The dog is charging at you and there is no handler visible. What should you do first?

choices.text: Approach the dog to calm it with your hand, to regain control before backup arrives.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Approaching an aggressive dog without a handler is dangerous.

choices.text: Back away slowly without running, avoid direct eye contact, and radio dispatch your exact location.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Slow withdrawal and communication protect your safety without provoking the animal.

choices.text: Run away as fast as possible, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Running can trigger a chase response and increase the risk of attack.

parts.prompt: The dog retreats to the loading bay. What should you do next?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Jan-14 Topi Obse Patro Deali Intera Traffi Post Starti Endin Work Use o Conc Refer Test 1. O a b 2. A a b 3. V a b 4. I n a b 5. A a b 6. False ndividuals w . True . F

choices.text: Enter the loading bay alone to close the door and secure the dog, to avoid getting stuck in a long interaction.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. Entering a confined space with an aggressive animal alone creates unnecessary risk.

choices.text: Document the incident, notify dispatch of the open bay and the dog, and request animal control if needed.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Documentation and notification ensure the hazard is addressed and logged for follow-up.

choices.text: Continue your patrol through the same area right away, to prevent delays in the response.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Incorrect. The hazard has not been resolved — continue only once the area is confirmed safe.
